justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with sequential p99 sum of 1150ms vs 500ms inbound. (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — upstream abandons at 500ms while downstream calls continue, wasting resources. (3) Outbound timeout (1000ms) > inbound timeout (500ms) relationship explicitly called out as 'fundamentally broken'. (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, increasing inbound timeout, reducing outbound timeouts with deadline budgeting, making InventoryService async/optional, caching, graceful degradation. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Went beyond the rubric by noting that even parallelized, InventoryService p99 alone (800ms) still exceeds the 500ms inbound timeout — showing deeper analysis than required
Excellent cascading failure analysis: connection pool exhaustion from orphaned requests under load is a real-world consequence often missed
Practical remediation options well beyond the minimum — includes context cancellation propagation, circuit breakers, deadline-aware retry policies, and liveness/readiness probe separation
Strong operational framing — the '3am on-call engineer' perspective grounds the observability gaps in real incident response
Correctly notes that adding retries before fixing the timeout budget would make things worse — shows systems thinking
Minor: output is verbose for a review — some sections (health check, database safety) add bulk without strengthening the core timeout cascade finding
